one_time_description: Treaty benefits claim against Government of Canada; expenses of $1,643,042 incurred in negotiations, financed by RBC loan and accounts payable
economic_notes: Waterhen Forestry Products LP (99.99% interest) is the government business enterprise accounted for by modified equity method; total revenue $5,852,722, net income $451,039. First Nation is a member of Meadow Lake Tribal Council (nine nations). Resort (North) and Resort (South) operations consolidated.
notes: Fiscal year is March 31, 2020. Three fully consolidated subsidiaries: Waterhen First Nation CMHC Housing Program, Waterhen Lake Resort (North) Ltd., Waterhen Lake Resort (South) Ltd. One GBE: Waterhen Forestry Products LP (modified equity). Outstanding treaty benefits claim against Government of Canada financed by RBC demand loan up to $2,988,160; $917,865 drawn as at March 31. First Nation in breach of financial statement submission deadline (audit dated November 10, 2020, past July 29, 2020 deadline). Also in breach of First Nations Bank covenant for timely submission of audited financials. CMHC reserves underfunded by $60,034. Total government transfers calculated as ISC $9,006,533 + Meadow Lake Health $3,364,028 + MLTC Program Services $2,435,296 + MLTC Child & Family Services $1,073,669 + CMHC $285,815 + Health Canada $35,000 + Department of Highways $162,232 + Investment income MLTC Resource Development LP $185,000 + Northern Lights CDC $235,331 + Province of Saskatchewan $4,500 + First Nations Trust $622,527 (partially government-related) = approximately $16,278,921 used as estimate; OSR includes retail sales, rental income, interest, other revenue, Mistik, GST rebates, PADC, earnings from business entity.
